{
  "report": "Rule Name: Building a command line with string concatenation
Rule Description: Using concatenated strings in a command line is vulnerable to malicious insertion of special characters in the strings.
Severity: error
Detailed Message: Command line is built with string concatenation.
Location-file: /activemq-tooling/activemq-perf-maven-plugin/src/main/java/org/apache/activemq/tool/sampler/plugins/LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin.java
Location-start-line: 55
Location-start-col: 55
Location-end-line: 55
Location-end-col: 60",
  "response": "{
  "judgement": "FP",
  "analysis": "Step 1 (B1): the sink `Runtime.getRuntime().exec(vmstat)` is a real process-execution sink, but the reported source is not a dangerous one in this flow. The command is constructed only as `\"vmstat -n \" + (int)(intervalInMs / 1000)`, so the variable part is an integer token, not attacker-controlled free-form text; it cannot carry shell metacharacters or inject another command. Also, Java `Runtime.exec(String)` does not invoke a shell, so shell syntax is not interpreted here. Step 2 (A1/D4 as secondary context): in the actual project workflow, `LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin` is only instantiated from `CpuSamplerTask.createPlugin()` using `getInterval()`, and `CpuSamplerTask` itself is created locally inside `AbstractJmsClientSystem.runSystemTest()` and immediately used. Although the Maven mojos accept keys with prefix `cpuSampler.`, those properties do not reach this plugin object graph because `ReflectionUtil` can only traverse nested properties through getters on the configured root object, and `AbstractJmsClientSystem` has no `getCpuSampler()`/`setCpuSampler()` property. Therefore the executed command is effectively a fixed internal command (`vmstat -n 1` by default), not a real CWE-094 issue in this project.",
  "FP_Reason": [
    "B1.Incorrect Source/Sink Selection"
  ],
  "evidence": "Code evidence: `LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin(long intervalInMs) { vmstat = \"vmstat -n \" + (int)(intervalInMs / 1000); }` and later `vmstatProcess = Runtime.getRuntime().exec(vmstat);` (`LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin.java` lines 33-35 and 55). The only dynamic fragment is numeric. Creation path: `plugin = new LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin(getInterval());` (`CpuSamplerTask.java` lines 38-39), where the inherited default is `protected long interval = 1000;` (`AbstractPerformanceSampler.java` line 41). Runtime flow: `CpuSamplerTask cpuSampler = new CpuSamplerTask(); ... cpuSampler.createPlugin();` (`AbstractJmsClientSystem.java` lines 84-90), with no configuration of that `cpuSampler` instance before plugin creation. Property-binding evidence: `ProducerMojo` forwards `cpuSampler.` keys (`ProducerMojo.java` lines 38-40), but `ReflectionUtil.configureClass` reaches nested properties only through getters such as `getXxx()` (`ReflectionUtil.java` lines 53-75); `AbstractJmsClientSystem` has no `getCpuSampler()` property, so `cpuSampler.vmstat` or `cpuSampler.interval` cannot bind to the created sampler/plugin. Repository search also shows no caller of `LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin.setVmstat(String)`.",
  "new_reason:": "There is a separate latent risk if some external embedding application directly instantiates `LinuxCpuSamplerPlugin` and calls `setVmstat(String)` with attacker-controlled data, but that is not the CodeQL-reported flow and is not reachable in this project's normal Maven/CLI execution path."
